summary.intent: Address five P2 findings from a Codex automated review (PR #696) on TreeView §5/§6 lazy-loading and rename functionality in the ars-ui Rust component library.
summary.outcome: All five findings fixed with TDD tests, specs updated (core + Leptos + Dioxus adapters), CI passed (178 tests, clippy clean), and changes committed and pushed to the PR branch.

repo: TreeView state machine lives in crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tree_view/mod.rs with a companion tests.rs; spec docs are in spec/components/navigation/tree-view.md plus adapter specs under spec/leptos-components/ and spec/dioxus-components/.

New callback props follow the pattern: add a Callback<dyn Fn(Event) + Send + Sync> field to Props, a corresponding EventStruct, an Effect variant, and a *_effect() helper — mirroring the existing on_reorder/ReorderEvent/Effect::Reorder pattern.
xci-fast (cargo xci --profile fast) is the authoritative CI gate; it runs 10 steps including lib tests, clippy, spec validate, and snippet compile. Must pass before committing.
Codex review results are polled via GitHub GraphQL reviewThreads API, filtering on isResolved==false and author login matching 'codex'.
TreeCollection::new accepts duplicate nodes; get() returns only the last copy, causing duplicate visible rows — stale ChildrenLoaded deliveries must be guarded at the state machine level.

code.finding: expanded_change_plan used .find() instead of iterating, so ExpandAll over multiple lazy branches only fired LoadChildren for the first one — fixed to collect all lazy keys and attach one Effect::LoadChildren per key.
needs_lazy_load only matched NotLoaded, blocking retries after LoadError — fixed to also accept Error state so re-expanding a failed branch retriggers the load.
on_props_changed did not compare renamable, so flipping it false mid-edit left renaming_key live — fixed by adding renamable to the comparison and clearing renaming_key in the SyncProps arm.
The existing load_error_then_reexpand_retries test was asserting error stickiness (the old wrong behavior) and needed updating to assert the retry instead.

workflow: Codex review findings should be addressed TDD-first: write/update the failing test, fix the impl, confirm green, then spec-sync and commit.
Commit replies to Codex threads should reference the commit SHA per project convention.
xci-fast should be kicked off as a background task while preparing the commit message to avoid idle waiting.
Spec files (core + both adapter specs) must be updated in the same commit as impl changes to keep them in sync.

friction: Locating the exact insertion points for new structs/fields/helpers required multiple grep+read passes — the file is large enough that a single read doesn't cover all relevant regions.
The Codex poll loop completed before the assistant read it, requiring a tail of the output file rather than live streaming.
The xci-fast invocation command in the transcript appears to have been run without a full path/alias resolution visible, suggesting the alias may not always be obvious to new contributors.
open_items: Codex may require a second review pass (re-trigger) after these fixes — the transcript ends at push without confirming all 5 threads were resolved.
Adapter implementations (Leptos, Dioxus) for the new on_rename prop were only spec-updated; actual adapter component code wiring on_rename was not shown and may still be needed.
The duplicate-node behavior in TreeCollection::new (get() returns last copy) is a latent footgun — a defensive assertion or deduplication in the collection constructor was not addressed.
